title: Prime Intellect raises $130M to help enterprises build their own AI agents
friendlyTitle: Prime Intellect raised money to help companies build their own AI agents
summary: Prime Intellect raised a $130 million Series A at a reported $1 billion valuation to provide compute, reinforcement-learning infrastructure, and evaluation tools for companies building their own AI agents. TechCrunch reports customers include Ramp and Zapier, with the startup positioning itself as a way for enterprises to refine models for proprietary workflows.
friendlySummary: Prime Intellect raised $130 million for tools that help businesses train, test, and run AI agents for their own work.
whyItMatters: More companies want AI systems they can adapt and control instead of sending every workflow to a frontier lab. The funding reflects demand for enterprise-owned agent infrastructure around data control, cost, reliability, and specialized performance.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Companies want useful AI without giving up control of important data and workflows. This is part of a shift toward owning more of the AI stack.
